data.title: Contel Technology Releases Audited Annual Results for FY2026
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Contel Technology Company Limited ( (HK:1912) ) has issued an announcement.

Contel Technology Company Limited has announced its audited consolidated annual results for the year ended 31 March 2026, confirming that the full 2026 annual report will be made available on both the company’s website and the Hong Kong Stock Exchange’s disclosure platform. The announcement underscores the group’s compliance with Hong Kong listing requirements for preliminary annual results disclosures and signals the forthcoming publication of detailed financial statements and governance reports for stakeholders.

The company’s board, comprising executive and independent non-executive directors, formally approved the release, with the company secretary noting that the annual report will include comprehensive sections such as management discussion and analysis, corporate governance, ESG reporting and a five-year financial summary. This structured disclosure gives investors and regulators a consolidated view of the group’s financial performance and oversight framework, supporting transparency and continued adherence to market rules.

More about Contel Technology Company Limited

Contel Technology Company Limited is a Hong Kong–listed company incorporated in the Cayman Islands with limited liability, operating through a group structure that includes various subsidiaries. The company is focused on technology-related businesses and is regulated under the Rules Governing the Listing of Securities on The Stock Exchange of Hong Kong Limited.

Average Trading Volume: 4,676,780

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

JL MAG Rare-Earth Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:6680) ) has issued an announcement.

JL MAG Rare-Earth Co., Ltd., a Chinese producer of high-performance rare-earth permanent magnets, supplies critical magnetic materials to sectors including new energy vehicles, wind power and industrial automation. The company leverages its rare-earth processing capabilities to support strategic industries that depend on stable, high-specification magnet solutions.

The group expects a strong interim performance for the first half of 2026, projecting net profit attributable to shareholders of RMB400 million to RMB460 million, up about 31% to 51% year on year. Profit after excluding non-recurring items is forecast to rise 57% to 83%, with second-quarter earnings also showing sharp gains, underscoring improved core profitability and signalling a solid operating momentum for investors despite the figures remaining unaudited.

More about JL MAG Rare-Earth Co., Ltd. Class H

data.title: CA Cultural Technology Group Boosts Margins but Posts Wider Annual Loss
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

CA Cultural Technology Group Ltd. ( (HK:1566) ) has issued an update.

CA Cultural Technology Group reported revenue of HK$360.4 million for the year ended 31 March 2026, a slight 1.8% decline from the prior year, but gross profit surged 35.9% to HK$121.1 million as cost of sales fell and gross margin improved to 33.6%. The company also recorded higher other income and reduced administrative expenses, reflecting efforts to tighten operations and improve profitability at the gross level.

Despite operational improvements, the group’s loss attributable to shareholders widened sharply to HK$99.9 million, more than triple the previous year, with basic loss per share rising to 8.5 HK cents. High finance costs of over HK$106 million and an overall rise in expenses such as selling, marketing and distribution weighed heavily on the bottom line, underscoring ongoing balance sheet and funding pressures for investors and other stakeholders.

More about CA Cultural Technology Group Ltd.

CA Cultural Technology Group Ltd. operates in the cultural and technology sector, providing products and services that generate revenue primarily from sales and related services in Hong Kong and surrounding markets. The group focuses on technology-driven cultural offerings, with its performance closely tied to cost management, financing structure and overall efficiency of operations.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

data.description:

The latest announcement is out from Nanjing Leads Biolabs Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:9887) ).

Nanjing Leads Biolabs has granted a total of 2,254,691 H share awards to 149 employees under its H Share Award Scheme, including executive director and CFO Mr. Zuo, with all awards satisfied by existing shares acquired on the market to avoid dilution of current shareholders. The awards, priced at nil with a reference market price of HK$49.32 per H share, are subject to structured vesting schedules and individual performance targets tied to functional benchmarks, and are designed to incentivize new and existing staff and align their contributions with the group’s long-term operational and growth objectives.

The vesting for Mr. Zuo will occur in three tranches between September 2026 and January 2028, while other employees’ awards will vest over 36 or 48 months, sometimes with shorter initial periods due to administrative delays. The board and remuneration committee state that the flexible vesting periods remain within scheme rules and market practice, aiming to enhance employee retention and motivation without altering the company’s capital base, which may strengthen Nanjing Leads Biolabs’ competitive position in attracting and rewarding key talent in the biopharmaceutical sector.

CITIC Securities Co ( (HK:6030) ) just unveiled an update.

CITIC Securities has called its third extraordinary general meeting of 2026 for 17 July in Beijing, where shareholders will vote on a proposed issuance of H shares to a specific investor and related resolutions. The agenda includes approving the share class, pricing, lock-up, proceeds use, listing venue and authorization for the board to handle the issuance, as well as endorsing a three-year shareholder return plan for 2026 to 2028.

Shareholders will also be asked to approve a conditional share subscription agreement with the specific investor and classify the transaction as a related-party or connected deal, which may influence the company’s capital structure and governance. China Development Bank Financial Leasing Co Ltd Class H ( (HK:1606) ) has issued an announcement.

China Development Bank Financial Leasing Co., Ltd., via its wholly owned subsidiary CDB Aviation Lease Finance DAC, is expanding its aircraft leasing portfolio as part of its broader focus on aviation, shipping, energy, high-end equipment and inclusive finance. The company operates as a qualified aircraft lessor under Hong Kong listing rules and emphasizes that its transactions are carried out on normal commercial terms in the ordinary course of business.

The group has agreed to acquire two Boeing 787-9 aircraft from Lufthansa Asset Management Leasing GmbH under purchase and leaseback arrangements, with delivery expected by mid-July 2026. Unity Group Holdings International Limited ( (HK:1539) ) has provided an update.

Unity Group Holdings International Limited reported a 24.6% drop in revenue to HK$118.7 million for the year ended 31 March 2026, as both energy-saving system provisions and product trading declined. Despite lower sales, gross profit fell by a smaller margin, down 14.5% to HK$79.9 million, reflecting resilient margins in its core energy-efficiency offerings.

Profitability metrics improved sharply, with EBITDA more than doubling to HK$101.3 million and net profit surging 68.2% to HK$66.6 million, supported by gains from modifications of financial assets and liabilities. The group’s adjusted profit before tax excluding major extraordinary items edged up to HK$37.0 million, while basic earnings per share nearly doubled, highlighting stronger bottom-line performance even as adjusted EPS softened, signalling a profit structure increasingly influenced by one-off financial gains.

More about Unity Group Holdings International Limited

Unity Group Holdings International Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company focused on energy-saving solutions, including the provision of energy-saving systems, trading of energy-saving products and related licensing. The group targets customers seeking efficiency and cost reductions, positioning itself within the broader energy efficiency and green technology market.

data.title: Junshi Biosciences Licenses Autoimmune Antibody Roconkibart to Fosun Wanbang for Greater China
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Shanghai Junshi Biosciences Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:1877) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Shanghai Junshi Biosciences has signed a licensing agreement with Fosun Wanbang, a subsidiary of Fosun Pharma, granting it exclusive rights to develop, register and commercialize the anti-IL-17A monoclonal antibody roconkibart (JS005) in Greater China, along with co-exclusive manufacturing rights. The deal includes an upfront payment of RMB215 million, up to RMB1.125 billion in development and sales milestones, and double-digit tiered royalties, strengthening Junshi’s monetization of its pipeline while leveraging Fosun’s commercial infrastructure.

The agreement secures long-term revenue potential for Junshi through extended royalty terms tied to patent and regulatory exclusivity, underscoring confidence in roconkibart’s prospects in autoimmune diseases such as psoriasis and ankylosing spondylitis. Roconkibart has demonstrated strong phase III efficacy and safety in moderate to severe psoriasis, and its NDA for adult plaque psoriasis has been accepted by China’s National Medical Products Administration, positioning the product as a potential new biologic competitor in the autoimmune segment and enhancing Junshi’s standing in the domestic biologics market.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1877) stock is a Hold
with a HK$34.70 price target.

Voyah Automotive Technology Co Ltd Class H ( (HK:7489) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Voyah Automotive Technology reported June 2026 deliveries of 14,223 vehicles, up 41% year-on-year, bringing year-to-date deliveries to 76,264 units, a 36% increase that underscores its momentum in China’s competitive new energy vehicle market. The company attributed part of this growth to its self-developed AI agents and large-model-based digital marketing system, which it says has lifted sales operation efficiency by about 30% and is being expanded across the full value chain.

At the same time, Voyah will begin mass deliveries of the all-electric Taishan X8, a large five-seat flagship with a 120kWh Amber battery-based pack, moves that broaden its product lineup and are intended to create new growth drivers in the premium EV segment.

data.title: TransThera Completes Global Phase III Enrollment for Tinengotinib in Advanced Cholangiocarcinoma
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

An update from TransThera Sciences (Nanjing). Inc. ( (HK:2617) ) is now available.

TransThera Sciences has completed patient enrollment in a global multicenter, randomized Phase III trial of its lead drug tinengotinib as an oral monotherapy for advanced cholangiocarcinoma with FGFR gene aberrations that has relapsed or become refractory after chemotherapy and prior FGFR inhibitor treatment. The study spans centers in the U.S., Europe and Asia, and compares tinengotinib with physician’s choice therapy, positioning the company to potentially address a high unmet need in a market projected to grow from US$2.0 billion in 2024 to US$3.2 billion by 2027.

Tinengotinib is currently the first and only investigational therapy worldwide in late-stage development for CCA patients who have failed both chemotherapy and FGFR inhibitors, supported by Orphan Drug and Fast Track designations from the U.S. FDA and Orphan Drug status from the EMA for biliary tract cancers. With prior trials showing favorable safety and anti-tumor activity, the successful progression of this registrational program could significantly enhance TransThera’s position in oncology, offering a potential new late-line option for heavily pretreated patients and strengthening its standing with regulators and investors.

More about TransThera Sciences (Nanjing). Inc.

TransThera Sciences (Nanjing), Inc. is a China-based biopharmaceutical company focused on discovering and developing innovative small-molecule therapies for solid tumors. Its core pipeline includes tinengotinib, a multi-kinase inhibitor targeting FGFRs, VEGFRs, mitotic kinases and JAK, with ongoing trials in cholangiocarcinoma, prostate, breast and liver cancers and regulatory fast-track support in major markets.

Average Trading Volume: 5,465,893

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$3.61B

China Resources and Transportation Group Limited reported a rise in revenue to HK$609.3 million for the year ended 31 March 2026, up from HK$577.7 million a year earlier, with gross profit increasing to HK$182.1 million. data.description:

SEM Holdings Limited ( (HK:9929) ) has issued an update.

SEM Holdings Limited has entered into a loan agreement with Hainan Hongyan Information Technology Co., Ltd., under which the lender will provide an unsecured, interest-free term loan of up to RMB200 million for six months starting 1 July 2026, renewable upon negotiation. The company plans to use the proceeds as general working capital to fund day-to-day operations and support business development.

The board says the facility’s nil interest rate and unsecured nature provide more favorable terms than prevailing market lending conditions, helping to reduce financing costs for the group. As the lender is controlled by chairman and non-executive director Yao Shuobin, the deal constitutes a connected transaction under Hong Kong listing rules, but it is fully exempt from shareholder approval, annual review and disclosure requirements because it is on normal commercial terms or better and not secured by group assets.

More about SEM Holdings Limited

SEM Holdings Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong, operating through its subsidiaries as part of the broader commercial sector. The group focuses on developing its business with an emphasis on maintaining adequate working capital to support expenditure in the ordinary course of operations and ongoing corporate development.

WuXi AppTec Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2359) ) has provided an update.

WuXi AppTec announced a change in its Hong Kong company secretarial and process agent arrangements, reflecting routine adjustments in its corporate governance structure. The move underscores the company’s ongoing compliance with Hong Kong listing and companies regulations, which is critical for maintaining its market standing and investor confidence.

The firm reported that Ms. Cheung Yuet Fan has retired from her roles as one of the company secretaries and as an authorised process agent in Hong Kong, with the board noting there was no disagreement or issues requiring shareholder attention. She is being replaced by Ms. Yeung Siu Wai Kitty, a seasoned corporate secretarial professional from Tricor Services Limited, who will jointly handle company secretarial duties and governance matters alongside Ms. Min Han.

data.title: Greenheart Group Outlines Board Composition and Governance Committees
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

Greenheart Group ( (HK:0094) ) has provided an announcement.

Greenheart Group has announced the current composition of its board, naming Ding Wai Chuen as the sole executive director and chief executive officer, alongside non-executive directors Conrad Cheng Chi-Him, Jacob Lee Chi Hin and Julia Suen Chung Yan, and independent non-executive directors Francis Wong Man Chung, Patrick Cheung Pak To and To Chun Wai. The company also detailed the membership of its audit, remuneration and nomination committees, underscoring its adherence to corporate governance norms and clarifying oversight responsibilities for investors and other stakeholders.

The audit and remuneration committees are both chaired by independent director Francis Wong, with Jacob Lee and To Chun Wai serving alongside him, while the nomination committee is chaired by independent director To Chun Wai and includes Patrick Cheung and Julia Suen. By formally disclosing these roles and functions, Greenheart signals a governance framework that separates management from board oversight, which can strengthen investor confidence and align the company with regulatory and best-practice expectations in Hong Kong’s capital markets.

More about Greenheart Group

Greenheart Group Limited is a Bermuda-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong, operating within the broader investment and resources sector. The group is overseen by a board comprising one executive director serving as chief executive officer, several non-executive directors, and a slate of independent non-executive directors, reflecting a typical governance structure for a mid-sized listed enterprise.

The latest update is out from Bairong, Inc. Class B ( (HK:6608) ).

Bairong Inc., incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong under stock code 6608, operates as a technology-driven financial services platform. The company is controlled through weighted voting rights and is led by Chairman and Chief Executive Officer Zhang Shaofeng, reflecting a governance structure common among fast-growing fintech players seeking strategic flexibility and founder control.

The company has updated investors on the composition of its board and four board committees, detailing the roles of executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors. By clarifying audit, remuneration, nomination and corporate governance committee memberships, Bairong signals a structured approach to oversight and compliance, which may support stakeholder confidence and align its governance practices with market and regulatory expectations.

This governance disclosure highlights the influence of independent directors in key oversight roles, with committee chairmanships concentrated among them. The move reinforces Bairong’s efforts to balance founder control with independent scrutiny, a dynamic that remains central to investor assessment of fintech issuers using weighted voting rights structures.
